summary: Relaxed June '76 show with soaring Peggy-O and epic Playing‑In‑The‑Band jam
blurb: The concert rolls out with a laid‑back, no‑hurry vibe, highlighted by a soaring rendition of Peggy‑O and a heartfelt Mission In The Rain finale. The second set builds into an epic Playing In The Band → The Wheel → Playing In The Band sequence and a tight Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen sandwich. Keith’s piano shines and the dual drummers lock in for solid, energetic grooves.
review: June 29, 1976 at Chicago’s Auditorium Theatre captured one of the Dead’s most relaxed yet inspired performances. From the opening “Tennessee Jed” through a beautifully sung “Peggy‑O,” the band moved with a gentle confidence that let each song breathe. Jerry’s guitar was on fire – his slide work and subtle wah‑wah phrasing gave “Mission In The Rain” an emotional punch, while his lead lines drove the monumental “Playing In The Band > The Wheel > Playing In The Band” jam into soaring heights. Keith Godchaux’s piano was described as magical, weaving lush fills that lifted the second‑set flow, especially during the Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen sandwich. Phil Lesh’s bass remained crystal clear and “slippery,” anchoring the grooves while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann delivered tight, energetic drumming that launched every jam. The show’s relaxed pacing, seamless segues, and standout moments make it a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead in peak ’76 form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All nine recordings are marked positive, with multiple reviewers praising the soaring Peggy‑O, the epic Playing In The Band > The Wheel > Playing sequence, Keith’s magical piano, and tight drumming. Consistent high praise across sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, highlighted performances (Peggy‑O, Mission In The Rain) and flawless audio that showcases the band’s relaxed vibe.

key_highlights: Epic Playing In The Band → The Wheel → Playing In The Band jam
Tight Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen sandwich
Keith Godchaux’s magical piano fills and tight dual‑drum groove

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring guitar tone, expressive slide work and dynamic wah‑wah phrasing; his vocals on Peggy‑O and Mission In The Rain are especially heartfelt.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is crystal‑clear and ‘slippery,’ providing a solid foundation that shines throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar stays tasteful, while Donna’s backing vocals stay in key and complement the mellow vibe.
band_performance.Keith: Godchaux’s piano is described as magical and on fire, weaving beautiful fills that lift the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart locks in with tight, energetic playing, launching “Not Fade Away” and driving the jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann provides a solid rhythmic backbone, keeping the grooves tight and propelling the extended improvisations.
